rendered_via: Blender MCP socket execute_code on port 9876

root_cause.issue: The earlier cleanup projected skin by a flat x/z face rectangle and ignored front-depth. Hoodie hood geometry shares the same x/z band as the face, so skin was applied onto the hood. The UV candidate also still has leg/skin tearing from atlas projection.
root_cause.fix_attempt: Only assign skin to face polygons that are in the face x/z band, face forward, and sit near the front-most negative-Y depth. Keep the hood on the original projected hoodie texture.

notes: This candidate confirms the owner's suspicion about skin being applied from the wrong angle/zone around the hoodie hood. It is still not production-integrated because leg texture artifacts remain.
